title: QUIC CRT trim and Native AOT compatibility work item
summary: Add trim and Native AOT compatibility to the shipped Incursa.Quic package on the supported net10.0 target, and prove that the package remains consumable through PackageReference under PublishTrimmed and PublishAot.
planned_changes:
- Enable trim and Native AOT analyzer coverage for the supported net10.0 library build.
- Audit the public API and internal QUIC helpers for reflection, runtime code generation, and other trim or AOT incompatible patterns.
- Add a downstream PackageReference consumer smoke path that publishes the package under trimming and Native AOT.
- Add a requirement-home proof that keeps the package boundary, analyzer warnings, and downstream publish results explicit.
- Keep unsupported dynamic-extension or plugin-based consumer models out of scope.
verification_plan: Build the library with trim and Native AOT analyzers enabled, then publish a downstream PackageReference consumer with PublishTrimmed and PublishAot and fail the slice on any trim or AOT warnings or runtime regressions. Re-run the repo-local SpecTrace validation checks after the artifact updates.
out_of_scope: unsupported plugin or reflection extension models
multi-target packaging beyond the supported net10.0 slice
completion_notes: The package compatibility slice now proves the shipped PackageReference boundary. The library packs on net10.0, the downstream consumer publishes successfully under both trimmed and Native AOT modes, and the supported surface remains limited to the package-compatible QUIC API without introducing unsupported reflection or runtime code generation.
